steps.title: Convert radians to degrees
steps.content:
To convert from radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180}{\pi}$.

$$\frac{\pi}{10} \text{ rad} \times \frac{180}{\pi} = \frac{180}{10} = 18°$$

So angle G measures 18 degrees.

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content:
We're told that angle G equals $9n$ degrees. Since we found that angle G = 18 degrees:

$$9n = 18$$

$$n = 2$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
